title: Caldera - Exfiltration to Web Service via curl (Linux)
description: Detects Linux-side data upload to public web services used as exfiltration channels. Caldera T1567 emulation uploads staged files to paste sites, cloud storage APIs (S3, GCS), or anonymous file-sharing services using curl. Covers the endpoint-level activity that OCI network monitoring may miss.
query: ('Log Source' = 'SOC Linux Syslog Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Secure Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Syslog Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Audit Logs') and (( ((msg like '*paste.ee*' or msg like '*pastebin.com*' or msg like '*ghostbin.co*' or msg like '*transfer.sh*' or msg like '*file.io*' or msg like '*ix.io*' or msg like '*0x0.st*')) or ((msg like '*s3.amazonaws.com*' or msg like '*storage.googleapis.com*' or msg like '*blob.core.windows.net*' or msg like '*objectstorage.oraclecloud.com*')) ) or ( ((msg like '*curl -X PUT*' or msg like '*curl -X POST*' or msg like '*curl -T *' or msg like '*curl --upload-file*')) and ((msg like '*paste.ee*' or msg like '*pastebin.com*' or msg like '*ghostbin.co*' or msg like '*transfer.sh*' or msg like '*file.io*' or msg like '*ix.io*' or msg like '*0x0.st*')) ))
falsepositives: Developers uploading code snippets to paste sites during troubleshooting
Authorized backup jobs to cloud object storage
